cwe: CWE-770 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits

threat.title: GraphQL Query Depth and Complexity Attacks Causing Server Overload
threat.description: GraphQL APIs without depth and complexity limits allow attackers to craft deeply nested or computationally expensive queries that exhaust server resources, database connections, and memory. These attacks exploit GraphQL's flexible query structure and circular references between types to create exponentially expensive operations that can bring down production services. According to Escape's 2024 State of GraphQL Security Report, 69% of GraphQL API services are susceptible to DoS attacks due to unrestricted resource consumption.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker analyzes GraphQL schema using introspection query to identify circular references (e.g., User -> Posts -> Author -> Posts)
2. Crafts deeply nested query exploiting circular relationships with 20+ levels of nesting
3. Adds field aliasing to multiply the number of resolver calls (e.g., user1: user(id:1), user2: user(id:1), ...user100)
4. Sends malicious query to unprotected GraphQL endpoint
5. Server executes thousands of database queries and resolver functions simultaneously
6. Server exhausts database connection pool, memory, and CPU resources
7. Legitimate users experience timeouts and service degradation
8. Entire service becomes unavailable requiring manual restart
prerequisites: GraphQL API endpoint is publicly accessible
No query depth limiting middleware configured
No query complexity analysis in place
No rate limiting or query cost analysis
Schema contains circular type references (common pattern)

cve_references.description: Denial of Service in graphql-js package due to insufficient checks when parsing large queries in OverlappingFieldsCanBeMergedRule.ts, allowing attackers to degrade system performance
Directus GraphQL Field Duplication DoS vulnerability triggered by duplicating fields in queries, leading to excessive server resource consumption
graphql-java Denial-of-Service vulnerability allowing attackers to craft queries that cause excessive memory and CPU consumption

real_world_breaches.impact: 69% of 160 surveyed GraphQL API services (110 services) were susceptible to DoS attacks via unrestricted resource consumption. 13,720 total issues identified with 4,527 classified as highly critical. Average of 87 issues per service.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of query depth limits, complexity analysis, and cost controls across production GraphQL APIs

real_world_breaches.incident: GitHub GraphQL API Rate Limiting Implementation
real_world_breaches.impact: GitHub implemented node-based query cost analysis after observing abusive queries causing performance degradation
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Complex nested queries against repository and user data causing database overload

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Deeply nested GraphQL query causing complete service outage via database connection exhaustion

security_research.title: Securing Your GraphQL API from Malicious Queries
security_research.key_finding: Query depth can quickly overwhelm backing data sources without guardrails. Circular references enable exponentially expensive queries through nesting.

security_research.title: OWASP GraphQL Cheat Sheet - Denial of Service Prevention
security_research.key_finding: By default, GraphQL query depth and amount can both be unlimited which may lead to DoS attacks. Setting limits on depth and amount is recommended though not natively supported.

security_research.title: The State of GraphQL Security 2024
security_research.key_finding: 69% of GraphQL services vulnerable to DoS. 13.4% of vulnerabilities are specific to GraphQL language. 33% had high-severity issues, 72% medium-severity.

security_research.title: Defending Against GraphQL Attacks: A Deep Dive
security_research.key_finding: Alias overloading combined with depth attacks creates multiplicative resource consumption. Single query can trigger 10,000+ database operations.

mitigations.title: Implement Query Depth Limiting with graphql-depth-limit
mitigations.description: Enforce maximum query depth limits to prevent deeply nested queries from executing. The graphql-depth-limit library provides a validation rule that rejects queries exceeding a specified depth threshold before they reach resolvers.
nist_csf: PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
DE.CM-1 - Network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Install graphql-depth-limit: npm install graphql-depth-limit
Add depth limit validation rule to Apollo Server configuration
Set appropriate max depth (typically 5-10 based on schema)
Test with nested query exceeding limit (should return error)
Monitor logs for depth limit violations
Adjust limit based on legitimate use cases

mitigations.title: Implement Query Complexity Analysis with graphql-validation-complexity
mitigations.description: Assign cost values to fields and types, then calculate total query cost before execution. Reject queries exceeding complexity threshold. This provides more granular control than depth limiting by accounting for resolver performance characteristics.
nist_csf: PR.IP-12 - A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented
DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Install graphql-validation-complexity: npm install graphql-validation-complexity
Add @cost directives to schema fields with realistic complexity values
Configure complexity rule with appropriate maximum (start with 1000)
Test simple query and verify complexity calculation in logs
Test complex nested query that should exceed limit
Analyze production query patterns and adjust costs accordingly
Monitor complexity metrics to identify expensive queries

mitigations.title: Implement Query Cost Analysis with Token Bucket Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Use persisted query cost budgets per user/IP with token bucket algorithm. Each query consumes tokens based on calculated cost. Users regenerate tokens over time, preventing sustained DoS while allowing burst traffic.
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - Network monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Deploy Redis instance for distributed rate limiting
Install graphql-query-complexity: npm install graphql-query-complexity
Implement token bucket plugin with Redis backend
Configure bucket parameters based on expected load
Test rate limiting with load testing tool (e.g., artillery)
Verify legitimate users can execute normal queries
Confirm abusive queries are blocked with appropriate retry-after
Monitor Redis memory usage and query cost metrics
Set up alerts for rate limit violations

indicators: Sudden spike in GraphQL query execution time (>5s average)
High number of queries with depth >10 levels
Repeated queries from single IP with identical deeply nested structure
Memory usage spikes correlating with GraphQL requests
Queries containing excessive field aliasing (>50 aliases)
Failed health checks on GraphQL service during query bursts

log_queries.query:
// Detect GraphQL queries with excessive depth or complexity
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where CsUriStem contains "/graphql"
| where ScStatus == 200 or ScStatus == 500
| extend QueryDepth = extract(@'"depth":(\d+)', 1, CsUserAgent)
| extend QueryComplexity = extract(@'"complexity":(\d+)', 1, CsUserAgent)
| where toint(QueryDepth) > 10 or toint(QueryComplexity) > 1000
| summarize
    QueryCount = count(),
    AvgTimeTaken = avg(TimeTaken),
    MaxTimeTaken = max(TimeTaken),
    UniqueQueries = dcount(CsUriQuery)
    by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where QueryCount > 10
| order by QueryCount desc

url.path: "/graphql" AND (
  (http.response.status_code: 500 AND error.message: (*timeout* OR *memory* OR *connection*))
  OR
  (http.response.body.errors.extensions.code: ("QUERY_DEPTH_EXCEEDED" OR "QUERY_COMPLEXITY_EXCEEDED" OR "RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"))
)

index=app_logs sourcetype=apollo_server 
| where uri_path="/graphql"
| eval is_malicious=if(
    (like(error_code, "%DEPTH_EXCEEDED%") OR 
     like(error_code, "%COMPLEXITY_EXCEEDED%") OR
     execution_time_ms > 5000 OR
     query_depth > 10),
    1, 0)
| where is_malicious=1
| stats 
    count as malicious_queries,
    avg(execution_time_ms) as avg_exec_time,
    max(query_depth) as max_depth,
    dc(query_hash) as unique_queries
    by client_ip, user_id
| where malicious_queries > 5
| sort -malicious_queries

service:graphql-api
@query_depth:>10 OR @query_complexity:>1000 OR @duration:>5000
status:(error OR warning)
| group by @network.client.ip
| count > 20

tools.name: GraphQL Voyager
tools.usage: Visualize schema to identify circular references that enable depth attacks

tools.name: Apollo Studio
tools.usage: Monitor query complexity, execution time, and error rates in production

tools.name: Sentry
tools.usage: Track GraphQL timeout errors and resource exhaustion exceptions
